title: NOC 74100 – Mail and parcel sorters and related occupations | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 74100 professionals are the high-speed engine behind Canada’s physical communication and e-commerce networks. As a TEER 4 occupation (formerly Skill Level C), these workers process and sort the millions of letters and parcels that move through post offices, mail processing plants, and internal corporate mailrooms every day. In 2026, as Canada continues to see record-breaking parcel volumes driven by a "Digital-First" economy, these sorters are classified as Essential Logistics Talent. For immigration, this role is a prime candidate for the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway and several Provincial Nominee Programs (PNP).

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Level4Major Group74 – Mail and message distribution, other transport equipment operators and related maintenance workersSub-Major Group741 – Mail and message distribution occupationsMinor Group7410 – Mail and message distribution occupationsNOC Code74100

common_job_titles: Postal Clerk - Mail and Parcel Sorting
duties: Sortation: Sort mail and parcels according to destination manually or using computerized sorting systems.
Routing: Bundle, label, bag, and route sorted mail to the proper delivery stream (air, road, or local carrier).
Equipment Operation: Operate material handling equipment, such as forklifts or automated carts, to load and unload postal trucks.
Database Management: (Mailroom Clerks) Receive and process incoming/outgoing mail; maintain address databases and produce personalized mailings.
Verification: Search directories to find correct addresses for redirected or incomplete mail; weigh packages to calculate and affix proper postage.
Facility Maintenance: Perform basic maintenance on print shop, mailing house equipment, and stamp-cancelling machines.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Training: On-the-job training is provided, ranging from several weeks for sorters to a few months for specialized machine operators.
Education: Some college office administration courses may be required for corporate mailroom roles.

Certifications:Reliability Status (Security Clearance) is required for those working with Canada Post or sensitive government mail.WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement for warehouse environments.
Reliability Status (Security Clearance) is required for those working with Canada Post or sensitive government mail.
WHMIS (Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System) is a standard safety requirement for warehouse environments.
Physical fitness for standing long periods and lifting (up to 50 lbs) is essential.
salary_prospects: Note: In 2026, experienced sorters in unionized federal roles (Canada Post) often earn annual salaries between $48,000 and $62,000, inclusive of comprehensive medical/dental benefits and pension plans.
job_outlook: The outlook is Moderate. While automation is replacing some entry-level manual sorting tasks, the sheer volume of parcels generated by the 2026 retail landscape ensures that "human-in-the-loop" sorters are still required to handle fragile, oversized, and non-standard packages. Projections from the Canadian Occupational Projection System (COPS) indicate that Retirements will be the primary source of job openings through 2033.
labour_market_demand: The demand for mail and parcel sorters in 2026 is Moderate to Good, with high concentrations in major logistics hubs like Toronto (Mississauga/Brampton), Vancouver, and Montreal. While traditional letter mail volumes have declined, the exponential growth in e-commerce parcel sorting has created a steady need for workers. According to 2026 Job Bank data, many regions are reporting a "Balanced" market, though "Shortage" conditions exist in the Northeast Region of Ontario due to the rapid expansion of fulfillment centers.
The E-commerce Boom: Work in some of the most advanced automated sorting facilities in North America, utilizing AI-driven scanning and robotic conveyor systems.
Stable Public & Private Sector Roles: Employment opportunities range from the Canada Post Corporation (federal public sector) to major global couriers like FedEx, UPS, and DHL.
High-Tech Integration: Gain experience with optical character recognition (OCR) systems, computerized mail-sorting system operators, and automated labeling machines.
Strategic PR Pathways: This TEER 4 role is a direct beneficiary of the New 2026 TR to PR Pathway, which targets 33,000 workers already in Canada who are contributing to essential supply chains.
pathways: New 2026 TR to PR Pathway: A dedicated stream for 33,000 workers already in Canada, specifically targeting transport, warehouse, and logistics workers.
Saskatchewan SINP (Hard-to-Fill Skills): A high-priority route for parcel sorters with a job offer in the province's growing logistics sector.
OINP In-Demand Skills Stream: Frequently targets workers in the transportation and warehousing sector for regions outside the Greater Toronto Area.
Rural Community Immigration Pilot (RCIP): A direct route for sorters willing to settle in regional logistics hubs across Canada.
